states.name: Off_Idle
states.description: Device is off (OnOff == FALSE). OnWithTimedOff Delayed Off countdown may be active when OffWaitTime > 0, OnTime != 0xFFFF, and OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF.

states.name: On_Idle
states.description: Device is on (OnOff == TRUE). OnWithTimedOff Timed On countdown may be active when OnTime > 0, OnTime != 0xFFFF, and OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF.

transitions.timing_requirements: Immediate on receipt of Off.
Immediate on receipt of Off.
Immediate rejection when OffOnly feature enabled.
Immediate rejection when OffOnly feature enabled.
On turns device on without clearing OffWaitTime when clearing condition not met.
On turns device on and clears OffWaitTime when both timers supported and OnTime is zero.
On keeps device on; OffWaitTime unchanged when clearing condition not met.
On keeps device on and clears OffWaitTime when both timers supported and OnTime is zero.
Immediate rejection when OffOnly feature enabled.
Immediate rejection when OffOnly feature enabled.
Toggle from off turns device on when OnTime is not supported.
Toggle from off turns device on and clears OnTime when supported.
Toggle from on turns device off; OffWaitTime unchanged when timers unsupported or OnTime not zero.
Toggle from on turns device off and clears OffWaitTime when timers supported and OnTime is zero.
Stores global scene once, disables further storage, and turns device off with effect.
Stores global scene, disables further storage, turns device off, and clears OnTime when supported.
Turns device off with effect; global scene not overwritten.
Turns device off with effect and clears OnTime; existing global scene preserved.
Command processed while already off without storing or overwriting global scene.
Command is discarded when GlobalSceneControl is TRUE.
Recalls stored global scene and sets GlobalSceneControl TRUE; OffWaitTime unchanged when clearing condition not met.

transitions.guard_condition: FeatureMap.LT == 1 && GlobalSceneControl == FALSE && OnTime_supported == 1 && OffWaitTime_supported == 1 && OnTime == 0
transitions.timing_requirements: Recalls global scene, sets GlobalSceneControl TRUE, and clears OffWaitTime when OnTime is zero and timers supported.
Command is discarded when GlobalSceneControl is TRUE, leaving device on.
While already on, recalls global scene and sets GlobalSceneControl TRUE; OffWaitTime unchanged when clearing condition not met.

transitions.guard_condition: FeatureMap.LT == 1 && GlobalSceneControl == FALSE && OnTime_supported == 1 && OffWaitTime_supported == 1 && OnTime == 0
transitions.timing_requirements: While already on, recalls scene, sets GlobalSceneControl TRUE, and clears OffWaitTime when timers supported and OnTime is zero.
Command discarded when AcceptOnlyWhenOn is set and device is off.

transitions.guard_condition: FeatureMap.LT == 1 && OnOffControl.AcceptOnlyWhenOn == 0 && OnOff == FALSE && OffWaitTime_supported == 1 && OffWaitTime > 0
transitions.timing_requirements: Implements Delayed Off guarded case: OffWaitTime reduced to minimum of current and field value.
Timed On behavior when previously off without positive OffWaitTime guarded-off period.
Timed On behavior when already on: OnTime and OffWaitTime updated.
Every 0.1 s while device is on and OnTime > 1, decrement OnTime by 1.
When OnTime reaches 1, next tick sets OnTime to 0, OffWaitTime to 0, and turns device off.
Every 0.1 s in Delayed Off, while OffWaitTime > 0, decrement OffWaitTime by 1.

scene_behaviors: When Lighting feature (LT) is present and OffWithEffect is invoked while GlobalSceneControl is TRUE and the device is on, the server calls store_global_scene(), sets GlobalSceneControl to FALSE, sets OnOff to FALSE, and clears OnTime when supported.

When OnWithRecallGlobalScene is invoked while LT is present and GlobalSceneControl is FALSE, the server calls recall_global_scene() on the Scenes Management cluster, which updates OnOff and other attributes as part of scene recall, and then sets GlobalSceneControl to TRUE. If OnTime is 0 and both OnTime and OffWaitTime are supported, OffWaitTime is cleared.
The OnOff attribute is scene-capable and can be stored and restored via the Scenes Management cluster, including as part of the global scene.

definitions.definition: Boolean attribute indicating whether the device is considered off (FALSE) or on (TRUE) from the perspective of the On/Off cluster.
definitions.usage_context: Used to distinguish Off_Idle and On_Idle states and to select branches in command and timer processing.

definitions.definition: Unsigned 16-bit attribute in 1/10 second units used by OnWithTimedOff to represent a period during which the device should remain on. Zero means no remaining timed-on period; 0xFFFF disables automatic decrement.
definitions.usage_context: Updated by Off, Toggle, OffWithEffect, OnWithRecallGlobalScene, and OnWithTimedOff, and decremented by timer transitions while OnOff is TRUE when both OnTime and OffWaitTime are finite.

definitions.definition: Unsigned 16-bit attribute in 1/10 second units used with OnWithTimedOff to guard the off state. Zero means there is no remaining guarded-off period; 0xFFFF disables automatic decrement.
definitions.usage_context: Updated by On, Toggle, Off, OffWithEffect, OnWithRecallGlobalScene, and OnWithTimedOff, and decremented by timer transitions while OnOff is FALSE when both timers are finite.

definitions.definition: Boolean attribute controlling when a global scene may be overwritten. When TRUE, the next OffWithEffect that turns the device off stores the global scene and then sets this attribute to FALSE to prevent overwriting; when FALSE, OffWithEffect does not store a new global scene.
definitions.usage_context: Used in guards for OffWithEffect and OnWithRecallGlobalScene transitions.

definitions.definition: Bitmap attribute indicating which optional On/Off cluster features are enabled on the server, including Lighting (LT), DeadFrontBehavior (DF), and OffOnly (OFFONLY).
definitions.usage_context: Used in guards for feature-dependent behavior, such as lighting commands and OffOnly restrictions.

definitions.definition: Cluster feature enabling lighting-specific attributes and commands, including GlobalSceneControl, OnTime, OffWaitTime, OffWithEffect, OnWithRecallGlobalScene, and OnWithTimedOff.
definitions.usage_context: Required in guards for transitions that rely on lighting semantics such as timed on and delayed off behavior.

definitions.definition: Cluster feature indicating only the Off command is supported; On and Toggle must be rejected with an UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND status.
definitions.usage_context: Used in guards for On and Toggle transitions to generate UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND without changing OnOff.

definitions.definition: 8-bit bitmap used as the OnOffControl field of the OnWithTimedOff command. It contains the AcceptOnlyWhenOn flag that can restrict command acceptance.
definitions.usage_context: The AcceptOnlyWhenOn bit is referenced in guards for OnWithTimedOff transitions from the off state.

definitions.definition: Bit within the OnOffControlBitmap that, when set to 1, restricts OnWithTimedOff so it is only accepted if the device is already on.
definitions.usage_context: Used in guards to discard OnWithTimedOff commands when the device is off and this bit is set.

definitions.definition: Abstract operation on the Scenes Management cluster that stores the current configuration as a special global scene identified by group 0 and scene 0.
definitions.usage_context: Invoked by OffWithEffect when GlobalSceneControl is TRUE and the device is on to preserve current settings before turning off.

definitions.definition: Abstract operation on the Scenes Management cluster that restores the previously stored global scene, updating attributes on the endpoint based on that scene.
definitions.usage_context: Invoked by OnWithRecallGlobalScene when GlobalSceneControl is FALSE to restore configuration and set GlobalSceneControl back to TRUE.

definitions.definition: Abstract action representing that the server responds to a command with an UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND status code without modifying application-visible attributes.
definitions.usage_context: Used in transitions where On or Toggle are invoked on a device with the OffOnly feature.

definitions.definition: Abstract boolean indicating that the OnTime attribute is implemented and writable on the server.
definitions.usage_context: Used when applying timer-related side effects only if OnTime exists.

definitions.definition: Abstract boolean indicating that the OffWaitTime attribute is implemented and writable on the server.
definitions.usage_context: Used in guards and helper logic for transitions that modify or inspect OffWaitTime, including OnWithTimedOff guarded-off behavior.

definitions.definition: Abstract helper that represents clearing the OnTime attribute to zero when the OnTime attribute is implemented; when OnTime is not implemented, it has no effect.
definitions.usage_context: Used in Off and OffWithEffect transitions to express conditional clearing of OnTime without embedding control flow in actions.

references.name: On/Off cluster
references.description: Defines OnOff attribute, commands Off, On, Toggle, OffWithEffect, OnWithRecallGlobalScene, OnWithTimedOff, and features LT, DF, OFFONLY that are modeled in this FSM.

references.name: Level Control cluster
references.description: Interacts with On/Off under Lighting feature to adjust OnOff, OnTime, and OffWaitTime; detailed level interactions are abstracted here.

references.name: Scenes Management server cluster
references.description: Provides global scene storage and recall used by OffWithEffect and OnWithRecallGlobalScene.

references.name: FeatureMap
references.description: Bitmap attribute containing LT, DF, and OFFONLY feature bits, used in guards for feature-dependent behavior.

references.name: ClusterRevision
references.description: Global attribute indicating cluster revision; ensures device behavior aligns with described revisions such as inclusion of FeatureMap and StartUpOnOff.

references.name: UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND status
references.description: Interaction Model error status returned when unsupported commands, such as On or Toggle under OffOnly, are invoked.

security_properties.description: When the OffOnly feature is enabled, On and Toggle commands never change OnOff and always result in UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND.
security_properties.formal_specification: For all transitions where FeatureMap.OFFONLY == 1 and trigger is command:On or command:Toggle, the post-state OnOff equals the pre-state OnOff and an action generate_status(UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND) occurs.
security_properties.attack_vector: A client may attempt to circumvent regulatory restrictions by issuing On or Toggle commands on an OffOnly device.
security_properties.mitigation: FSM enforces guards that block OnOff changes and generate UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND whenever OFFONLY is set.

security_properties.description: If AcceptOnlyWhenOn is set and the device is off, OnWithTimedOff has no effect on attributes.
security_properties.formal_specification: For all transitions with trigger command:OnWithTimedOff where OnOffControl.AcceptOnlyWhenOn == 1 and OnOff == FALSE in the pre-state, OnOff, OnTime, and OffWaitTime in the post-state equal their pre-state values.
security_properties.attack_vector: A client could try to pre-configure timed-on behavior while the device is off despite AcceptOnlyWhenOn being set.
security_properties.mitigation: FSM includes a transition from Off_Idle that discards the command under these conditions without attribute changes.

security_properties.description: When OnWithTimedOff uses finite OnTime and OffWaitTime, the Timed On and Delayed Off countdowns follow the specified decrement rules for OnTime and OffWaitTime.
security_properties.formal_specification: In any run where OnTime != 0xFFFF and OffWaitTime != 0xFFFF, all timer:OnWithTimedOff_tick transitions satisfy: if OnOff == TRUE and OnTime > 1, the post-state OnTime equals pre-state OnTime minus 1 and OffWaitTime is unchanged; if OnOff == TRUE and OnTime == 1, the post-state has OnTime == 0, OffWaitTime == 0, and OnOff == FALSE; if OnOff == FALSE and OffWaitTime > 0, the post-state OffWaitTime equals pre-state OffWaitTime minus 1 and OnTime and OnOff are unchanged.
security_properties.attack_vector: An implementation might incorrectly omit the Delayed Off countdown or mishandle timer decrements, causing unexpected guarded-off durations.
security_properties.mitigation: FSM contains explicit timer transitions for both the Timed On and Delayed Off phases that decrement only the intended attribute in each phase.

security_properties.description: Toggle always clears OnTime when the resulting OnOff is TRUE and clears OffWaitTime when the resulting OnOff is FALSE and OnTime is zero with timers supported, without relying on extra non-protocol triggers.
security_properties.formal_specification: For all command:Toggle transitions where FeatureMap.OFFONLY == 0, if the post-state OnOff == TRUE and OnTime_supported == 1 then post-state OnTime == 0; if the post-state OnOff == FALSE, OnTime_supported == 1, OffWaitTime_supported == 1, and post-state OnTime == 0 then post-state OffWaitTime == 0.
security_properties.attack_vector: Inconsistent treatment of timers on Toggle could leave stale OnTime or OffWaitTime values that influence future behavior unexpectedly.
security_properties.mitigation: FSM embeds the timer side effects directly into the Toggle transitions’ actions and does not use any artificial post-command triggers or hidden previous state variables.

security_properties.description: When GlobalSceneControl is FALSE, OffWithEffect never stores a new global scene.
security_properties.formal_specification: In all transitions with trigger command:OffWithEffect where GlobalSceneControl == FALSE in the pre-state, no action store_global_scene() occurs.
security_properties.attack_vector: A malicious or buggy client might repeatedly call OffWithEffect to overwrite a stored global scene even when scene protection should be active.
security_properties.mitigation: FSM restricts store_global_scene() to OffWithEffect transitions where GlobalSceneControl is TRUE and simultaneously sets GlobalSceneControl to FALSE so it cannot be repeatedly overwritten.
